page: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. It is equipped with 192 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM running at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core.

The instance features 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-capacity, low-latency local storage. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which minimizes virtualization overhead to deliver near-bare-metal performance. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps, supporting high-throughput data transfers.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8id.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance. It achieves top 10% placement in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth tests, with cached read speeds reaching 7652.04 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier results in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests. The hardware also excels in prompt processing for large language models. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads, high-performance database hosting, large-scale software compilation, and real-time data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 192 GB of DDR5 RAM and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 40 Gbps baseline network speed. The system delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as demonstrated by strong benchmark results in Redis SET operations and Passmark CPU tests. With hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX and AMX, this instance is optimized for high-performance computing, database workloads, software compilation, and large language model prompt processing. The dedicated CPU allocation and high-speed memory architecture ensure consistent performance for demanding enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-performance database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-compute x86 server featuring ninety-six virtual cores, rapid DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
192 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations via AVX and AMX extensions
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
